# Jiko — instant decision making instrument

> A physical decision-making instrument by [p → q]. You hold the button, speak one intention, and three independent readings — content, emotion, and context — lock into red, yellow, or green signals. It does not tell you what to do. The decision stays with you.

## What It Is

Jiko is a small physical instrument (pre-release v0.9). It runs a single ritual: hold, speak, observe three signals, hear one spoken line, then silence. It reveals whether your content, emotion, and context agree or diverge — nothing more.

## What It Is Not

- Not an AI assistant or recommendation engine
- Not a chatbot or therapist
- Not a traffic light that says stop or go

## Three Readings

| Public Term | Code Channel | What It Reads |
|-------------|-------------|---------------|
| Content | `"text"` | What the user said |
| Emotion | `"voice"` | How the user sounded |
| Context | `"timing"` | When the user hesitated, rushed, or paused |

## Signal States

- **Maintain** (Red) — inertia, return to familiar path
- **Deviate** (Green) — rupture, break from pattern
- **Static** (Yellow) — cannot form stable signal
